question: Describe the key components of the Entity-Relationship (ER) model and explain the purpose of each component.
expectedPoints: Entities: Represent real-world objects or concepts.
Attributes: Properties or characteristics of an entity.
Relationships: Associations between two or more entities.

question: Compare and contrast the hierarchical and network data models, highlighting one key advantage and one key limitation of each.
expectedPoints: Hierarchical Model: Advantage - Simplicity and data integrity; Limitation - Rigid structure and complexity to represent many-to-many relationships.
Network Model: Advantage - Ability to represent complex relationships; Limitation - High complexity for programmers and users.
Both are legacy models largely replaced by the relational model.

question: What is the primary purpose of a data model in a Database Management System?
expectedPoints: To provide a conceptual representation of how data is to be structured and organized.
To hide the complexities of physical storage details from users.
To serve as a blueprint for designing a database.
